assertion_results.text: Output maps ValidationError to 400 Bad Request
assertion_results.evidence: Answer maps ValidationError to HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity — exactly the opposite of the correct mapping

assertion_results.text: Output maps InvalidDataError to 422 Unprocessable Entity
assertion_results.evidence: Answer does not assign an HTTP code to InvalidDataError; it describes it as semantic/business-rule failure but gives no explicit HTTP status

assertion_results.text: Output states ValidationError belongs in application or infrastructure layers
assertion_results.evidence: Answer says ValidationError 'belongs closer to the boundary/input parsing layer' — vague and not explicitly application or infrastructure

assertion_results.text: Output states InvalidDataError belongs in domain entities, value objects, or builders
assertion_results.evidence: Answer says InvalidDataError 'belongs in the domain or application layer' — incorrectly includes the application layer

assertion_results.text: Output clearly distinguishes the two errors by layer and purpose
assertion_results.evidence: Answer inverts HTTP codes and conflates layers for both errors; the distinction is present but the mapping is wrong
